calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by zoom for Kathleen Newsom (Administrator).

The Court waives notice of the supplemental declaration and asked Ms. Harrington if she wants to add anything regarding the Probate Examiner's corrections to the statutory compensation. Ms. Harrington stated that she has no issue with the correction. The Second Amended Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-29-2019 Continued To Mar-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Kathleen Newsom (Special Administrator).

The Court noted there is no response to the Probate Examiner's notes that were sent on November 13, 2018. Ms. Harrington stated that she is not sure if she received the notes, that she will respond to them and that they have been looking for the family tree to show the Court that they have served everyone. Ms. Harrington also stated that they dealt with claiming the body from the morgue, that they retained a Genealogist to do a search in Europe and that person is still working on it, and that they are still cleaning out the apartment. In addition, Ms. Harrington requested a 60-day continuance stating that there are three bank accounts, that the largest one is a pay on death account, that the other two are non pay on death accounts, that they are larger than a small estate and that there is substantial personal property, but it appears to be of no significant value. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Harrington will file an ex parte petition to have the special letters extended.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
